title: Operators
description: Perform calculations and comparisons with operators

blocks.content: Operators are symbols that tell JavaScript to perform an operation on one or more values. Common types include arithmetic, comparison, and logical operators.

Use these to perform math:
- `+` Addition
- `-` Subtraction
- `*` Multiplication
- `/` Division
- `%` Modulus (remainder)
- `**` Exponentiation (power)

These compare values and return `true` or `false`:
- `==` Equal to (loose equality)
- `===` Equal to (strict equality, recommended)
- `!=` Not equal to
- `!==` Not equal to (strict)
- `>` Greater than
- `<` Less than
- `>=` Greater than or equal
- `<=` Less than or equal

blocks.code:
console.log(5 === 5);      // true
console.log(5 === "5");    // false (different types)
console.log(5 == "5");     // true (values are same, ignores type)
console.log(5 !== 5);      // false
console.log(10 > 3);       // true
console.log(10 < 3);       // false
console.log(10 >= 10);     // true

blocks.title: Use === Not ==
blocks.content: Always use `===` (strict equality) instead of `==`. It's safer and prevents unexpected type conversions.

These combine boolean values:
- `&&` AND (true only if both are true)
- `||` OR (true if at least one is true)
- `!` NOT (flips true to false and vice versa)

These assign or update values:
- `=` Assign
- `+=` Add and assign
- `-=` Subtract and assign
- `*=` Multiply and assign
- `/=` Divide and assign

blocks.code:
let score = 10;
score += 5;  // score = score + 5, now 15
score -= 3;  // score = score - 3, now 12
score *= 2;  // score = score * 2, now 24
console.log(score); // 24
